name: Mango-Habanero HOT Sauce
intro: THIS THICK CONDIMENT provides a nice balance of sweet, savory, and spicy. The flavor is fantastic, and the combination of habanero, mango, and carrot gives the sauce a striking color. Although I made it specifically for Crunchy Bean and Okra Fritters, it would go well with all types of foods, from breakfast burritos to roasted russet potatoes. In the instructions I suggest adjusting the amount of habanero chile depending on the amount of heat you want. For even less heat, remove the seeds and veins before mincing the habanero (it’s a good idea to wear gloves to do this). Even if the sauce is quite hot initially, I find that it tends to mellow after a few days.
method: Warm the oil in a small saucepan over medium-low heat. Add the onion, carrot, cumin, salt, and cayenne and sauté until the onion just starts to brown, 8 to 10 minutes. Add the garlic, chile, and lime zest and sauté until the garlic is fragrant, 2 to 3 minutes. Add the mango, water, vinegar, lime juice, and white pepper and sauté until everything is warmed through, 2 to 3 minutes.
Transfer to a blender and puree until smooth. Taste and season with more salt if desired. Stir in the parsley. Use immediately or store in a tightly sealed jar in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.
